message.content:
Read-only survey of the TypeScript MCP server at /worksp/monitor/app/apps/nas-mcp and the shared registry at /worksp/monitor/app/packages/shared/src. I'm about to add 5 file-inventory MCP tools (and later 7 archive-move tools) that hit native nas-api job endpoints instead of the /exec path. Report COMPACT findings (signatures, names, shapes, line refs) — do not paste whole files.

Answer each:
1. `packages/shared/src/nas-tools.ts`: show the exact TypeScript interface for a tool definition (field names + types — e.g. name, description, write, params, buildCommand). Is `buildCommand` required or optional? Show the shape of `ALL_TOOL_DEFS` (array of that interface) and how `TOOL_GROUPS` is structured. How are params declared (zod? show one example tool def verbatim, ideally a write tool).
2. `apps/nas-mcp/src/index.ts`: find the function that executes a predefined tool per NAS — where it calls buildCommand → /preview → /exec. Show that dispatch flow (the key lines / function name). Where would a `if (tool.job) {...}` native branch be inserted? Also confirm the `EAGER_TOOLS` list.
3. `apps/nas-mcp/src/nas-client.ts`: show `getNasConfigs`/`NasConfig` — the field names (apiSecret, approvalSigningKey, url, etc.) and which env vars they read (NAS_EDGE1_API_URL etc.). Show how an approval token is currently built/signed in nas-mcp (buildApprovalToken or equivalent) — the exact HMAC string and base64url payload shape.
4. `apps/nas-mcp/tools-config.json`: show the top-level structure (enabled_read_tools / enabled_write_tools arrays) and roughly how many entries each has.
5. Confirm the tsconfig strictness and the build command (package.json "name" field + build script) for nas-mcp and for packages/shared.

Tight conclusions only.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

47	function readOnlySql(value: string): string {
48	  const sql = value.trim().replace(/;+\s*$/, "");
49	  if (!sql || sql.length > 2000) {
50	    throw new Error("SQL query must be a non-empty read-only statement under 2000 characters.");
51	  }
52	  if (/[;\x00]/.test(sql) || /--|\/\*/.test(sql)) {
53	    throw new Error("SQL query must be a single read-only statement without comments.");
54	  }
55	  if (/\b(attach|detach|insert|update|delete|replace|create|drop|alter|vacuum|reindex|analyze|load_extension|pragma\s+(?!table_info\b|table_xinfo\b|index_list\b|index_info\b|database_list\b|foreign_key_list\b))/i.test(sql)) {
56	    throw new Error("Only SELECT/WITH and safe schema PRAGMA queries are allowed.");
57	  }
58	  if (!/^(select|with)\b/i.test(sql) && !/^pragma\s+(table_info|table_xinfo|index_list|index_info|database_list|foreign_key_list)\s*\(/i.test(sql)) {
59	    throw new Error("Only SELECT, WITH, or safe schema PRAGMA queries are allowed.");
60	  }
61	  return sql;
62	}

64	/**
65	 * Builds a shell command that makes a single authenticated DSM WebAPI call.
66	 * Handles login, the API call, result check, and logout.
67	 * Requires DSM_USERNAME and DSM_PASSWORD in the container environment (.env).
68	 */
69	function buildDsmApiCall(
70	  api: string,
71	  version: number,
72	  method: string,
73	  extraArgs: string[], // each entry is a --data-urlencode "key=value" fragment (value may reference $SHELL_VAR)
74	  description: string,
75	): string {
76	  return [
77	    `if [ -z "\${DSM_USERNAME:-}" ] || [ -z "\${DSM_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then echo "ERROR: DSM_USERNAME/DSM_PASSWORD not set in .env — required for WebAPI calls"; exit 1; fi`,
78	    `DSM_BASE="http://localhost:\${DSM_PORT:-5000}/webapi/entry.cgi"`,
79	    `echo "=== Authenticating to DSM WebAPI ==="`,
80	    `SID=$(curl -sfG "$DSM_BASE" --data-urlencode "api=SYNO.API.Auth" --data-urlencode "version=7" --data-urlencode "method=login" --data-urlencode "account=\${DSM_USERNAME}" --data-urlencode "passwd=\${DSM_PASSWORD}" --data-urlencode "format=sid" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '"sid":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)`,
81	    `if [ -z "$SID" ]; then echo "ERROR: DSM login failed — check DSM_USERNAME/DSM_PASSWORD and port \${DSM_PORT:-5000}"; exit 1; fi`,
82	    `echo "Authenticated"`,
83	    `echo ""`,
84	    `echo "=== ${description} (${api} v${version} method=${method}) ==="`,
85	    `RESULT=$(curl -sfG "$DSM_BASE" --data-urlencode "api=${api}" --data-urlencode "version=${version}" --data-urlencode "method=${method}" ${extraArgs.join(" ")} --data-urlencode "_sid=$SID" 2>/dev/null)`,
86	    `echo "$RESULT"`,
87	    `echo "$RESULT" | grep -q '"success":true' && echo "OK" || echo "Non-success — check DSM for errors"`,
88	    `echo ""`,
89	    `curl -sfG "$DSM_BASE" --data-urlencode "api=SYNO.API.Auth" --data-urlencode "version=7" --data-urlencode "method=logout" --data-urlencode "_sid=$SID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true`,
90	    `echo "Session closed"`,
91	  ].join("\n");
92	}

94	/**
95	 * Builds a shell command that restarts a DSM package via the local WebAPI.
96	 * Requires DSM_USERNAME and DSM_PASSWORD in the container environment (.env).
97	 * Validator classifies this as Tier 2 because it matches the SYNO.Core.Package
98	 * write-method pattern.
99	 */
100	function buildDsmPackageRestart(packageId: string): string {
101	  return [
102	    `if [ -z "\${DSM_USERNAME:-}" ] || [ -z "\${DSM_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then echo "ERROR: DSM_USERNAME/DSM_PASSWORD not set in .env — required for WebAPI package restarts"; exit 1; fi`,
103	    `DSM_BASE="http://localhost:\${DSM_PORT:-5000}/webapi/entry.cgi"`,
104	    `echo "=== Authenticating to DSM WebAPI ==="`,
105	    `SID=$(curl -sfG "$DSM_BASE" --data-urlencode "api=SYNO.API.Auth" --data-urlencode "version=7" --data-urlencode "method=login" --data-urlencode "account=\${DSM_USERNAME}" --data-urlencode "passwd=\${DSM_PASSWORD}" --data-urlencode "format=sid" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '"sid":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)`,
106	    `if [ -z "$SID" ]; then echo "ERROR: DSM login failed — check DSM_USERNAME/DSM_PASSWORD and that DSM is reachable on port \${DSM_PORT:-5000}"; exit 1; fi`,
107	    `echo "Authenticated to DSM"`,
108	    `echo ""`,
109	    `echo "=== Stopping ${packageId} via SYNO.Core.Package method=stop ==="`,
110	    `STOP=$(curl -sfG "$DSM_BASE" --data-urlencode "api=SYNO.Core.Package" --data-urlencode "version=1" --data-urlencode "method=stop" --data-urlencode "id=${packageId}" --data-urlencode "_sid=$SID" 2>/dev/null)`,
111	    `echo "$STOP"`,
112	    `echo "$STOP" | grep -q '"success":true' && echo "Stop: OK" || echo "Stop: non-success (package may already be stopped)"`,
113	    `echo ""`,
114	    `sleep 4`,
115	    `echo "=== Starting ${packageId} via SYNO.Core.Package method=start ==="`,
116	    `START=$(curl -sfG "$DSM_BASE" --data-urlencode "api=SYNO.Core.Package" --data-urlencode "version=1" --data-urlencode "method=start" --data-urlencode "id=${packageId}" --data-urlencode "_sid=$SID" 2>/dev/null)`,
117	    `echo "$START"`,
118	    `echo "$START" | grep -q '"success":true' && echo "Start: OK" || echo "Start: non-success — check DSM Package Center for errors"`,
119	    `echo ""`,
120	    `curl -sfG "$DSM_BASE" --data-urlencode "api=SYNO.API.Auth" --data-urlencode "version=7" --data-urlencode "method=logout" --data-urlencode "_sid=$SID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true`,
121	    `echo "Session closed"`,
122	  ].join("\n");
123	}

3475	export function searchTools(query: string, enabled: Set<string>): McpToolDef[] {
3476	  const lower = query.toLowerCase().trim();
3477	  if (!lower) return [];
3478	  const words = lower.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean);
3479	
3480	  // For each query word, build the set of matching tool names independently.
3481	  // - Direct group name (e.g. "files", "recovery"): only tools in that group, no description fallback.
3482	  // - KEYWORD_TO_GROUPS alias (e.g. "snapshot"): tools in the mapped groups + name/description match.
3483	  // - No mapping: name/description match only.
3484	  const perWord: Set<string>[] = words.map((word) => {
3485	    const matching = new Set<string>();
3486	    if (KNOWN_GROUPS.has(word)) {
3487	      for (const tool of ALL_TOOL_DEFS) {
3488	        if (enabled.has(tool.name) && getGroup(tool.name) === word) matching.add(tool.name);
3489	      }
3490	    } else {
3491	      const mappedGroups = new Set<string>(KEYWORD_TO_GROUPS[word] ?? []);
3492	      for (const tool of ALL_TOOL_DEFS) {
3493	        if (!enabled.has(tool.name)) continue;
3494	        if (
3495	          mappedGroups.has(getGroup(tool.name)) ||
3496	          tool.name.toLowerCase().includes(word) ||
3497	          tool.description.toLowerCase().includes(word)
3498	        ) {
3499	          matching.add(tool.name);
3500	        }
3501	      }
3502	    }
3503	    return matching;
3504	  });
3505	
3506	  // AND semantics: a tool must match every query word.
3507	  let candidates = perWord[0];
3508	  for (let i = 1; i < perWord.length; i++) {
3509	    const next = new Set<string>();
3510	    for (const name of candidates) {
3511	      if (perWord[i].has(name)) next.add(name);
3512	    }
3513	    candidates = next;
3514	  }
3515	
3516	  // Score for ordering within the intersection (name hit outweighs description hit).
3517	  const toolMap = new Map<string, McpToolDef>(ALL_TOOL_DEFS.map((t) => [t.name, t]));
3518	  const scored: { tool: McpToolDef; score: number }[] = [];
3519	  for (const name of candidates) {
3520	    const tool = toolMap.get(name)!;
3521	    let score = 0;
3522	    const nameLower = tool.name.toLowerCase();
3523	    const descLower = tool.description.toLowerCase();
3524	    for (const word of words) {
3525	      if (nameLower.includes(word)) score += 3;
3526	      if (descLower.includes(word)) score += 1;
3527	    }
3528	    scored.push({ tool, score });
3529	  }
3530	  scored.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score || a.tool.name.localeCompare(b.tool.name));
3531	  return scored.map((s) => s.tool);
3532	}

3534	function describeZodParam(schema: z.ZodTypeAny): string {
3535	  let inner: z.ZodTypeAny = schema;
3536	  let optional = false;
3537	  let defaultVal: unknown;
3538	  // Unwrap ZodOptional / ZodDefault chains
3539	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
3540	  while ((inner as any)?._def?.typeName === "ZodOptional" || (inner as any)?._def?.typeName === "ZodDefault") {
3541	    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
3542	    const d: any = (inner as any)._def;
3543	    if (d.typeName === "ZodOptional") optional = true;
3544	    if (d.typeName === "ZodDefault") {
3545	      try { defaultVal = d.defaultValue?.(); } catch { /* ignore */ }
3546	    }
3547	    inner = d.innerType;
3548	  }
3549	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
3550	  const tn: string | undefined = (inner as any)?._def?.typeName;
3551	  let type = "unknown";
3552	  if (tn === "ZodString") type = "string";
3553	  else if (tn === "ZodNumber") type = "number";
3554	  else if (tn === "ZodBoolean") type = "boolean";
3555	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
3556	  else if (tn === "ZodEnum") type = ((inner as any)._def.values as string[]).map((v) => JSON.stringify(v)).join(" | ");
3557	  else if (tn === "ZodArray") type = "array";
3558	  else if (tn === "ZodObject" || tn === "ZodRecord") type = "object";
3559	
3560	  const flags: string[] = [];
3561	  if (optional) flags.push("optional");
3562	  if (defaultVal !== undefined) flags.push(`default=${JSON.stringify(defaultVal)}`);
3563	  const flagStr = flags.length ? ` (${flags.join(", ")})` : "";
3564	  const desc = schema.description ? `  // ${schema.description}` : "";
3565	  return `${type}${flagStr}${desc}`;
3566	}
